steps.title: Identify the key signal
steps.content: Text 1 sets up an expectation: warming should benefit invasive species over native species (based on non-boreal research). Text 2 tests this in Alaska and finds that warming had no effect on either species' growth patterns across years of temperature variation.

steps.title: Match the finding to the choices
steps.content: Choice D correctly identifies the relationship: the Alaskan findings cast doubt on whether the non-boreal research generalizes to boreal contexts. The other choices mislabel it—A as methodology, B as an alternative invasion cause, C as research invalidity rather than inapplicability. That matches choice D—D is correct.
